#!/usr/bin/env pwsh
# CLI wrapper around the ArtifactCleanup module. Reads a JSON fixture of
# artifacts, applies the given retention policies, and prints a deterministic
# plan summary to stdout. Used both locally and from the GitHub Actions workflow.

[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string]$FixturePath,
    [int]$MaxAgeDays = 0,
    [long]$MaxTotalSizeBytes = 0,
    [int]$KeepLatestPerWorkflow = 0,
    [switch]$DryRun
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

Import-Module (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'ArtifactCleanup.psm1') -Force

if (-not (Test-Path $FixturePath)) {
    throw "Fixture not found: $FixturePath"
}

# Fixtures store CreatedAt as ISO-8601 strings so they're stable on disk.
$raw = Get-Content -LiteralPath $FixturePath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
$artifacts = foreach ($a in $raw) {
    [pscustomobject]@{
        Name          = $a.Name
        SizeBytes     = [long]$a.SizeBytes
        CreatedAt     = [datetime]::Parse($a.CreatedAt, [cultureinfo]::InvariantCulture, [System.Globalization.DateTimeStyles]::AssumeUniversal)
        WorkflowRunId = $a.WorkflowRunId
    }
}

# Policy parameters are optional; only forward the ones the caller actually set.
$policy = @{}
if ($MaxAgeDays            -gt 0) { $policy.MaxAgeDays            = $MaxAgeDays }
if ($MaxTotalSizeBytes     -gt 0) { $policy.MaxTotalSizeBytes     = $MaxTotalSizeBytes }
if ($KeepLatestPerWorkflow -gt 0) { $policy.KeepLatestPerWorkflow = $KeepLatestPerWorkflow }

$deleted = [System.Collections.ArrayList]::new()
$result = Invoke-ArtifactCleanup -Artifacts @($artifacts) -DryRun:$DryRun `
    -Deleter { param($a) [void]$deleted.Add($a.Name) } @policy

# Stable, line-oriented output that the harness greps against.
Write-Output "DRY_RUN=$($result.DryRun)"
Write-Output "DELETED_COUNT=$($result.Summary.DeletedCount)"
Write-Output "RETAINED_COUNT=$($result.Summary.RetainedCount)"
Write-Output "SPACE_RECLAIMED=$($result.Summary.SpaceReclaimedBytes)"
Write-Output "RETAINED_SIZE=$($result.Summary.RetainedSizeBytes)"
Write-Output "DELETE_NAMES=$((($result.Delete | ForEach-Object Name) | Sort-Object) -join ',')"
Write-Output "RETAIN_NAMES=$((($result.Retain | ForEach-Object Name) | Sort-Object) -join ',')"
Write-Output "ACTUALLY_DELETED=$(($deleted | Sort-Object) -join ',')"

name: Artifact Cleanup Script

# Run on push/PR to validate the script, on a daily schedule to actually clean
# artifacts up, and manually so a developer can dry-run any fixture.
on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 3 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fixture:
        description: 'Fixture file under fixtures/'
        required: false
        default: 'case1-max-age.json'

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  # Test harness selects which fixture + policy to run by writing this file.
  CASE_FILE: .ci-case.txt

jobs:
  cleanup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Show environment
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $PSVersionTable.PSVersion | Out-String
          Get-Module -ListAvailable Pester | Select-Object Name,Version | Out-String

      - name: Run unit tests (Pester)
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $r = Invoke-Pester -Path ./tests -PassThru -Output Detailed
          if ($r.FailedCount -gt 0) { throw "Pester: $($r.FailedCount) failed" }

      - name: Run cleanup script against fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # The harness writes .ci-case.txt with: fixture|maxAge|maxSize|keepN|dryRun
          # When absent (e.g. on plain push), default to case1 in dry-run.
          $caseFile = $env:CASE_FILE
          if (Test-Path $caseFile) {
            $line = (Get-Content -LiteralPath $caseFile -Raw).Trim()
          } else {
            $line = 'case1-max-age.json|30|0|0|true'
          }
          $parts = $line -split '\|'
          $fixture, $age, $size, $keep, $dry = $parts
          Write-Host "::group::Running case $fixture"
          $params = @{
            FixturePath           = "fixtures/$fixture"
            MaxAgeDays            = [int]$age
            MaxTotalSizeBytes     = [long]$size
            KeepLatestPerWorkflow = [int]$keep
          }
          if ($dry -eq 'true') { $params.DryRun = $true }
          ./Invoke-Cleanup.ps1 @params
          Write-Host "::endgroup::"

input.content:
#Requires -Module Pester

# End-to-end pipeline tests. Each Pester test case runs the workflow inside a
# disposable temp git repo via `act push --rm`, captures the output, and asserts
# on exact expected lines emitted by Invoke-Cleanup.ps1. All output is appended
# to act-result.txt in the project root.

BeforeAll {
    $script:ProjectRoot = (Resolve-Path (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot '..')).Path
    $script:ResultLog   = Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot 'act-result.txt'
    if (Test-Path $script:ResultLog) { Remove-Item -LiteralPath $script:ResultLog -Force }

    function script:Invoke-ActCase {
        param(
            [string]$Label,
            [string]$Fixture,
            [int]$MaxAge = 0,
            [long]$MaxSize = 0,
            [int]$Keep = 0,
            [bool]$DryRun = $false
        )

        # Build a fresh temp repo with the project files + the per-case .ci-case.txt
        # so this run is hermetic.
        $work = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) ("act-cleanup-" + [guid]::NewGuid().ToString('N'))
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $work | Out-Null
        Copy-Item -Path (Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot '*') -Destination $work -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path (Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot '.github') -Destination $work -Recurse -Force
        if (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot '.actrc')) {

name: Artifact Cleanup Script

# Run on push/PR to validate the script, on a daily schedule to actually clean
# artifacts up, and manually so a developer can dry-run any fixture.
on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 3 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fixture:
        description: 'Fixture file under fixtures/'
        required: false
        default: 'case1-max-age.json'

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  # Test harness selects which fixture + policy to run by writing this file.
  CASE_FILE: .ci-case.txt

jobs:
  cleanup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Show environment
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $PSVersionTable.PSVersion | Out-String
          Get-Module -ListAvailable Pester | Select-Object Name,Version | Out-String

      - name: Run unit tests (Pester)
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $r = Invoke-Pester -Path ./tests -PassThru -Output Detailed
          if ($r.FailedCount -gt 0) { throw "Pester: $($r.FailedCount) failed" }

      - name: Run cleanup script against fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # The harness writes .ci-case.txt with: fixture|maxAge|maxSize|keepN|dryRun
          # When absent (e.g. on plain push), default to case1 in dry-run.
          $caseFile = $env:CASE_FILE
          if (Test-Path $caseFile) {
            $line = (Get-Content -LiteralPath $caseFile -Raw).Trim()
          } else {
            $line = 'case1-max-age.json|30|0|0|true'
          }
          $parts = $line -split '\|'
          $fixture, $age, $size, $keep, $dry = $parts
          Write-Host "::group::Running case $fixture"
          $params = @{
            FixturePath           = "fixtures/$fixture"
            MaxAgeDays            = [int]$age
            MaxTotalSizeBytes     = [long]$size
            KeepLatestPerWorkflow = [int]$keep
          }
          if ($dry -eq 'true') { $params.DryRun = $true }
          ./Invoke-Cleanup.ps1 @params
          Write-Host "::endgroup::"
